question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getSidecar()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a crafted sidecar name via a malicious record file in the stream bucket such that `StreamFilename.from(recordFilename, sidecar.getName())` resolves to a path outside the intended sidecar directory, causing path traversal when `streamFileProvider.get(sidecarFilename)` fetches the file?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getSidecar()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a sidecar file where both `sidecar.getHash()` and `sidecar.getActualHash()` are null, causing `Arrays.equals(sidecar.getHash(), sidecar.getActualHash())` to return true and bypassing the hash integrity check entirely, allowing tampered sidecar content to be accepted?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getSidecar()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the non-constant-time `Arrays.equals(sidecar.getHash(), sidecar.getActualHash())` comparison to mount a timing side-channel attack that leaks the expected sidecar hash byte-by-byte, enabling hash forgery without triggering `HashMismatchException`?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getSidecar()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a sidecar file whose `sidecar.getName()` contains path separator characters (e.g., `../`) so that `StreamFilename.from(recordFilename, sidecar.getName())` constructs a `bucketFilePath` pointing to an arbitrary location in the storage bucket, causing `streamFileProvider.get()` to fetch unintended files?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getSidecar()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a sidecar file with an extremely large payload such that `sidecarFileReader.read(sidecar, streamFileData)` loads the entire content into memory via `sidecar.setBytes(bytes)`, causing an out-of-memory condition and denial of service in the importer process?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: '] [Function: downloadSidecars()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a record file that declares an unbounded number of `SidecarFile` entries in `recordFile.getSidecars()`, causing `Flux.fromIterable(recordFile.getSidecars()).flatMap(sidecar -> getSidecar(...))` to issue an unbounded number of concurrent network requests, exhausting connection pool resources and causing denial of service?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: '] [Function: downloadSidecars()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a record file with sidecars containing an enormous number of `TransactionSidecarRecord` entries such that `flatMapIterable(SidecarFile::getRecords)` and the subsequent `collect(Multimaps.toMultimap(...))` accumulate all records in an unbounded `ArrayListMultimap` in memory, causing heap exhaustion?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: '] [Function: downloadSidecars()] Can an unprivileged external user craft multiple sidecar records with identical `consensusTimestamp` values so that `Multimaps.toMultimap(TransactionSidecarRecord::getConsensusTimestamp, ...)` maps all of them to the same key, causing `recordItem.setSidecarRecords(records.get(timestamp))` to assign an arbitrarily large list of sidecar records to a single transaction item, leading to memory exhaustion or data corruption?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: '] [Function: downloadSidecars()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the condition `!downloaderProperties.isWriteFiles() && !sidecarProperties.isEnabled()` being evaluated with operator precedence ambiguity (missing parentheses around the OR with `recordFile.getSidecars().isEmpty()`) to bypass the early-return guard and force sidecar processing when it should be skipped, leading to unintended resource consumption?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getSidecar()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a sidecar file where `sidecar.getHash()` is a zero-length byte array and `sidecar.getActualHash()` is also zero-length, causing `Arrays.equals()` to return true and accepting a completely empty or zeroed hash as valid, bypassing integrity verification?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getSidecar()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a `NullPointerException` inside the reactive `Mono` chain in `getSidecar()` by supplying a sidecar whose `sidecar.getHash()` is null while `sidecar.getActualHash()` is non-null, causing `Arrays.equals(null, actualHash)` to return false and throwing `HashMismatchException` with a null expected hash that may be logged in a way that leaks internal state?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getSidecar()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a sidecar file such that `sidecarFileReader.read(sidecar, streamFileData)` throws an unchecked `InvalidStreamFileException`, which propagates through the `Mono` chain in `getSidecar()` and is not caught by `downloadSidecars()`, causing the entire `Flux.block()` to throw and aborting processing of all subsequent sidecars in the batch?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getSidecar()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a sidecar file with a crafted filename that, when passed to `importerProperties.getArchiveDestinationFolderPath(streamFileData)` and then `Utility.archiveFile(streamFileData.getFilePath(), sidecar.getBytes(), streamPath)`, resolves to a path outside the intended archive directory, causing arbitrary file write on the importer host filesystem?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getSidecar()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a sidecar filename shorter than 10 characters such that `importerProperties.getArchiveDestinationFolderPath()` calls `streamFileData.getFilename().substring(0, 10)` and throws `StringIndexOutOfBoundsException`, crashing the importer's sidecar archival path and causing denial of service?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: '] [Function: download()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `shouldDownload()` unconditionally returns `true` (unlike other downloaders that check `downloaderProperties.isEnabled()`) to prevent the importer from ever disabling the record downloader via the `endDate` cutoff mechanism, causing it to continue polling indefinitely and consuming resources?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: '] [Function: download()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a stream of record files that causes `cutoverService.getLastRecordFile().ifPresent(rf -> lastStreamFile.set(Optional.of(rf)))` to overwrite a more recent `lastStreamFile` with a stale value from `CutoverService`, causing the downloader to re-download already-processed files and potentially re-process duplicate transactions?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: '] [Function: setStreamFileIndex()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a record file with a spoofed version number less than `ProtoRecordFileReader.VERSION` (e.g., version 5) to force `super.setStreamFileIndex(recordFile)` to be called, overwriting the block_number embedded in the protobuf with an incorrect sequential index, causing block number desynchronization in the database?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: '] [Function: setStreamFileIndex()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a v6+ record file with a `block_number` field set to `Long.MAX_VALUE` or a negative value in the protobuf, bypassing the `setStreamFileIndex` override (which skips index assignment for v6+), causing integer overflow or invalid block index to be stored in the database?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: '] [Function: onVerified()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a record file with sidecars that cause `downloadSidecars()` to throw an unhandled exception before `super.onVerified()` is called, leaving `lastStreamFile` in an inconsistent state where the file is considered verified by the signature check but its sidecar data is never committed, causing data integrity gaps?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: '] [Function: onVerified()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a record file whose sidecar processing in `downloadSidecars()` succeeds but whose `super.onVerified()` subsequently fails, resulting in sidecar records being assigned to `RecordItem` objects via `recordItem.setSidecarRecords()` but the parent `RecordFile` never being notified to `streamFileNotifier.verified()`, causing orphaned sidecar data?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getSidecarType()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a `TransactionSidecarRecord` with an unrecognized `sidecarRecordsCase` value such that the `default` branch in `getSidecarType()` calls `Utility.handleRecoverableError()` and returns `SidecarType.SIDECAR_TYPE_UNKNOWN_VALUE` (0), and if `acceptedTypes` is empty (accept-all mode), this unknown sidecar type passes the filter in `downloadSidecars()` and is assigned to a `RecordItem`, injecting unvalidated data?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getSidecarType()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a `TransactionSidecarRecord` with an unknown `sidecarRecordsCase` that triggers `Utility.handleRecoverableError()` repeatedly across many records, flooding the importer logs with recoverable error messages and causing log-based denial of service or log injection if the `consensusTimestamp` value is attacker-controlled?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: '] [Function: downloadSidecars()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a record file where `sidecarProperties.getTypeOrdinals()` returns an empty set (default configuration), causing the filter `acceptedTypes.isEmpty()` to evaluate to true and bypassing all type-based filtering, so that any sidecar type including unknown/future types is accepted and processed without validation?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: '] [Function: downloadSidecars()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a record file with sidecars whose `sidecar.getTypes()` list is empty, causing `sidecar.getTypes().stream().anyMatch(acceptedTypes::contains)` to return false when `acceptedTypes` is non-empty, silently skipping the sidecar download even though it may contain relevant data, leading to incomplete data ingestion?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: '] [Function: downloadSidecars()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a record file with a sidecar whose declared `types` list contains a type ordinal matching `acceptedTypes` but whose actual content is of a different type, bypassing the pre-download type filter in `downloadSidecars()` while the post-download filter `acceptedTypes.contains(getSidecarType(t))` may still pass if the actual type also matches, injecting unexpected sidecar content?
